title: carex nigra subsp. broteroana
intro: Carex nigra subsp. broteroana, also known as black sedge, is a type of sedge plant native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion. The health benefits of Carex nigra subsp. broteroana include its potential to alleviate bloating and gas, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote healthy digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
